title: Kaizen, phase one: the frame
summary: Stand up the opt-in kaizen writer — the agent and skill that turn what your builds keep repeating into better stack packs — and wire it to run after each delivery, writing packs as commits you review. This first phase builds the frame only; sharpening rules and suggesting new packs come in later phases.
motivation: Give karta a place to learn from finished builds: an opt-in agent that improves your stack packs and never weakens a gate on its own.

included: the kaizen writer agent and the skill that dispatches it
the .karta/kaizen.json opt-in switch, its schema, and an example
post-delivery wiring in karta-deliver and a note in karta-build
the plugin validator and the agent-sync generator
the three plugin manifests and the generated Codex projections
README, AGENTS.md, and a how-to guide
excluded: the enforcing flag on stack packs and the gate behavior that honors it (ships as one slice with phase three, before any release)
reading KARTA-SME-OVERRIDE markers to sharpen packs (phase two)
the plain 'this rule is eroding' education notes (phase two)
spotting gaps and suggesting brand-new packs (phase three)
editing karta's built-in packs, and kaizen touching code, tests, the binder, or prose docs

design_facts.stack: Claude Code + Codex plugin repo: markdown skills and agents plus Python stdlib helper scripts (PEP-723, run via uv). No pyproject, no test framework, no CI — the before-commit gates are manual local scripts.

work_items.title: Add the kaizen writer agent and its skill
work_items.summary: Create the kaizen agent and the skill that dispatches it, plus the opt-in switch's schema and example and the validator check for it. On the first enabled run the skill copies every pack the project uses into the project's own pack folder; then kaizen writes and commits packs for you to review, and everything it says to a person goes through the plain-language skill.

contract.exposes: The karta-kaizen skill (dispatches the writer agent, seeds the project's packs into .karta/sme/ on the first enabled run, commits pack edits in delivery mode and hands them back in ad-hoc mode) and the karta-kaizen agent (writes only inside .karta/sme/ and its own config/report area). The opt-in file .karta/kaizen.json { enabled: boolean, focus?: string } — same shape as doc-gardner's switch, schema-checked by the validator. The agent's charter records the core rule: kaizen writes knowledge but never changes what gates a build; the advisory-pack mechanics arrive with phase three.

signals: capability escalation: adds a second write-capable agent (workspace-write Codex sandbox) that commits files; review that it stays confined to .karta/sme/ and its own config/report area

sensitive zone: edits the repo's own gate scripts (validate_plugin.py, sync_codex_agents.py); the kaizen.json check should mirror the doc-gardner one, and a missing BUNDLE_SITE mapping is a hard error
blast radius: about twenty paths, though most are generated projections regenerated by the sync scripts

oracle.command: uv run scripts/validate_plugin.py --self-test && uv run scripts/check_shared_copies.py --self-test && uv run scripts/sync_codex_agents.py --check && uv run scripts/sync_codex_skills.py --check
assertions: The four before-commit gates pass clean.
The Claude marketplace lists skills/karta-kaizen, and skills/karta-kaizen/agents/openai.yaml exists with a display_name.

sync_codex_agents.py maps karta-kaizen to its spawn-site skill in BUNDLE_SITE and regenerates .codex/agents/karta-kaizen.toml (workspace-write, derived from the agent's Write/Edit tools) and the bundled karta-kaizen.agent.md.
validate_plugin.py schema-checks a present .karta/kaizen.json the way it checks doc-gardner.json — enabled must be boolean, only enabled and focus keys allowed.

The skill documents seeding every used pack into .karta/sme/ on the first enabled run, committing pack edits in delivery mode, and routing all human-facing output through the karta-plainlanguage skill.
The Codex mirrors under .agents/skills and plugins/karta for karta-kaizen are byte-identical to the canonical skill.

work_items.title: Run kaizen after a delivery when it's turned on
work_items.summary: Wire karta-deliver to run kaizen after the doc-gardner phase when the opt-in switch is on, seeding packs on the first run and reporting what it did; add the matching note to karta-build for direct single-item runs. When the switch is off, nothing runs.

contract.exposes: A post-delivery kaizen phase in karta-deliver, placed after the doc-gardner phase and gated by .karta/kaizen.json, plus an equivalent note in karta-build for direct invocations. The delivery run report carries kaizen's outcome next to doc-gardner's.
contract.consumes: The karta-kaizen skill and the .karta/kaizen.json switch from the kaizen-agent-and-skill item.

signals: capability escalation: adds an automatic post-delivery step that lets kaizen write and commit stack packs on the integration branch; review that it runs only when opted in, runs after doc-gardner, and never halts the delivery

oracle.command: uv run scripts/validate_plugin.py --self-test && uv run scripts/check_shared_copies.py --self-test && uv run scripts/sync_codex_agents.py --check && uv run scripts/sync_codex_skills.py --check
assertions: The four before-commit gates pass clean.
karta-deliver runs the kaizen skill after the doc-gardner phase only when .karta/kaizen.json has enabled: true, seeds packs on the first enabled run, and no-ops when the file is absent or disabled.
The delivery run report includes kaizen's outcome next to doc-gardner's.
karta-build carries the equivalent kaizen note for a direct single-item invocation.
The Codex mirrors for karta-deliver and karta-build are regenerated and in sync.

work_items.title: Release the new version across the manifests
work_items.summary: Bump the plugin version from 1.12.0 to 1.13.0 in both plugin manifests and the marketplace metadata, keeping the two plugin manifests equal, and regenerate the manifest projection.
work_items.contract: All three manifests — .claude-plugin/plugin.json, .codex-plugin/plugin.json, and the marketplace metadata — read version 1.13.0, and the generated Codex install projection of the plugin manifest matches the canonical one.

oracle.command: uv run scripts/validate_plugin.py --self-test && uv run scripts/check_shared_copies.py --self-test && uv run scripts/sync_codex_agents.py --check && uv run scripts/sync_codex_skills.py --check
assertions: The four before-commit gates pass clean.
Both .claude-plugin/plugin.json and .codex-plugin/plugin.json read version 1.13.0 and stay equal to each other, as the validator enforces.
The generated plugins/karta/.codex-plugin/plugin.json projection matches the canonical manifest.

work_items.title: Document kaizen for readers
work_items.summary: Write the how-to guide for kaizen and note the new writer agent in the README and AGENTS.md — the second writer after doc-gardner, the one that edits your stack packs. Write it all in plain language.
work_items.contract: docs/how-to/kaizen.md explains turning kaizen on, the seed-all step, and review-by-commit; README.md and AGENTS.md name kaizen as the second writer agent, the one that edits the project's stack packs.

assertions: The validator self-test passes, so repo integrity is intact.
docs/how-to/kaizen.md explains turning kaizen on, the seed-all step, and that kaizen writes packs as commits you review.
README.md and AGENTS.md note kaizen as the second writer agent after doc-gardner, the one that edits the project's stack packs.
All new and edited prose reads in plain language.
